title: Goldman Turns Bearish on Enphase, Slashes Price Target to $32
text: Goldman Sachs analyst Brian Lee downgraded Enphase Energy from Buy to Sell rating while sharply reducing price target to $32 from $77 on July 8, 2025. Downgrade reflected reassessment of residential solar sector following recent policy changes in latest budget bill. BWG Global downgraded stock to Negative from Mixed View citing slowdown in company sales based on channel checks expecting Q1 2025 microinverter shipments could come below projections. Mizuho changed price target from $50 to $37 on October 23, 2025 representing significant downgrade. Primary concerns driving downgrades included weaker demand for solar solutions, falling operating margins and tariff impacts on earnings. Weak European market and heightened U.S. competition also pressured ratings. Analyst ratings downgraded from Moderate Buy to Hold in late 2024 continuing to deteriorate through Q2 2025. 22 analysts covering ENPH maintain consensus Hold rating with average price target of $40.66 forecasting -15.31% stock decrease over next year.
